method_atom: A scheme for assigning algebraic-topological invariants to a discretely structured object by building a groupoid that encodes the object's local equivalence and translation relations, then defining the object's cohomology as the cocycle cohomology of that groupoid. The objects analyzed are generated by a cut-and-project construction — taking a slice through a higher-dimensional periodic lattice and projecting it into a lower-dimensional subspace — and the invariant is computed explicitly as a function of the codimension of that construction.
object_atom: Aperiodic point patterns and tilings produced by the projection method, specifically canonical projection-method tilings of codimension at most 3, the subject matter of the mathematics of quasicrystalline / aperiodic order.
brief_reason: The groupoid-cohomology-as-invariant framework and the cut-and-project generating construction are transferable abstract machinery, while the specific aperiodic point patterns / canonical projection tilings of bounded codimension are the domain-specific objects.
